edges.description: JAK-STAT activation drives megakaryocyte proliferation and platelet production
Elevated platelet production leads to platelet dysfunction and thrombotic/hemorrhagic complications
Increased megakaryopoiesis produces sustained elevation of platelet number.
Clonal megakaryocytic proliferation increases megakaryocyte number in bone marrow.
ET megakaryocytic proliferation includes large mature megakaryocytes with hyperlobulated nuclei.
Megakaryocytic expansion and atypical marrow architecture produce abnormal bone marrow morphology.
Abnormal platelet activation and elevated platelet burden increase arterial thrombotic events.
Abnormal platelet activation and elevated platelet burden increase venous thrombotic events.
Platelet dysfunction and acquired von Willebrand physiology can prolong bleeding despite thrombocytosis.
Platelet dysfunction causes hemorrhagic complications despite elevated platelet counts.
Arterial thrombosis risk in ET can manifest as myocardial infarction.

nodes.description: Hemorrhagic complications include gastrointestinal bleeding, epistaxis, and easy bruising, particularly in those with extreme thrombocytosis.

Bone marrow cellularity and architecture are abnormal, with megakaryocytic hyperplasia, loose clustering of enlarged megakaryocytes, and characteristic absence of significant reticulin fibrosis distinguishing ET from prefibrotic myelofibrosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005561 | Abnormality of bone marrow cell morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Arterial thrombotic events are the major complication, including stroke, myocardial infarction, and peripheral arterial occlusion.
Constitutive JAK-STAT signaling drives increased megakaryocyte proliferation and maturation. Unlike myelofibrosis, megakaryocytes in ET are large and mature without significant atypia or clustering.
Bone marrow biopsy shows markedly increased megakaryocytes that are large and mature with hyperlobulated nuclei.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005513 | Increased megakaryocyte count | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Driver mutations in JAK2, CALR, or MPL converge on constitutive JAK-STAT signaling activation. JAK2 V617F directly activates the kinase; CALR mutations cause aberrant activation of MPL receptor; MPL mutations cause constitutive thrombopoietin receptor activation.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnostic reassessment in myeloproliferative neoplasms: the value of functional iron parameters and JAK2 allelic burden.
evidence.snippet: Polycythemia vera (PV) and essential thrombocythemia (ET) are chronic myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs), often associated with mutations in JAK2, CALR, and MPL.

nodes.description: Megakaryocytes in ET characteristically show hyperlobulated nuclei, a key histologic feature distinguishing ET from prefibrotic myelofibrosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0031388 | Megakaryocyte nucleus hyperlobulation | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Myocardial infarction is a major arterial thrombotic complication of ET.

Despite elevated platelet counts, thrombosis risk is multifactorial and involves platelet activation, leukocyte-platelet interactions, and endothelial dysfunction. Paradoxically, extreme thrombocytosis (>1,000,000/uL) may cause acquired von Willebrand disease and bleeding.
Prolonged bleeding time reflects platelet dysfunction despite elevated counts, particularly in patients with extreme thrombocytosis and acquired von Willebrand disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003010 | Prolonged bleeding time | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Persistent platelet count of 450,000/uL or greater is a diagnostic criterion. Many patients have counts exceeding 1,000,000/uL at diagnosis.
Venous thromboembolism including deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, and splanchnic vein thrombosis.
